summary:
Hypofractionated radiotherapy provides comparable oncologic outcomes to conventional therapy while significantly improving tolerance in pediatric patients with DIPG due to its lower fractionation schedule. This results in enhanced patient safety and quality of life during treatment despite limited impact on long-term survival. 

Note: Do not include your own interpretation or opinion; only summarize what was stated directly from the provided text.
